title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. rhipiphyllus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. rhipiphyllus, also known as the white water crowfoot, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
